headline: CSCO Stock Slipped ~4% This Week Even as AI Infrastructure Orders Surged
summary: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) shares are trading around $77, down 4.7% over the past week, even as the business story looks stronger than it has in years. Cisco posted $15.35 billion in Q2 FY2026 revenue, up 10% year over year, and management guided for full-year revenue of $61.2 billion to $61.7 billion, which would be the ... CSCO Stock Slipped ~4% This Week Even as AI Infrastructure Orders Surged

headline: Wall Street Backs Arista Networks as AI Data Center Play
summary: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) received a fresh vote of confidence from Wall Street as Susquehanna initiated coverage with a Positive rating and a $160 price target. The call arrives as Arista stock trades near $126.68, well below its 52-week high of $164.94, making the initiation a timely signal for investors watching the data center networking space. ... Wall Street Backs Arista Networks as AI Data Center Play

headline: Ciena's WaveLogic 6 Gains Traction With Vodafone Idea Deployment
summary: CIEN's WL6e is being leveraged by Vodafone Idea to deliver 1.6 Tb/s speeds, boosting capacity and lowering cost per bit amid surging AI and cloud demand.

headline: Oracle layoffs will help cost savings, analysts say
summary: Oracle plans to cut thousands of jobs which will free up cash flow as the company aggressively invests in its AI data center buildout.

raw.headline: Oracle layoffs will help cost savings, analysts say

headline: Why Arista Networks (ANET) Is Down 6.1% After Upgrading 2026 Outlook On AI Data Center Demand
summary: In recent months, Arista Networks reported exceptionally strong 2025 results driven by AI Center demand, raised its 2026 revenue growth outlook, and gained fresh analyst coverage highlighting its role in high-speed AI data center networking. NVIDIA’s US$2 billion investment in Marvell and expansion of the NVLink Fusion ecosystem also indirectly lifts Arista, as its Etherlink AI networking platform participates in the open NVLink Fusion framework and could benefit from broader third-party...
